title: Auto-Categorisation on Import
summary: Automatically assign a category to imported transactions based on description matching. Matching strategy and UX approach still open.

title: Resilient / Format-Agnostic Transaction Import
summary: Make the import pipeline durable against bank export format changes. Minimum: handle small variations without code fixes. Ambitious: fully format-agnostic importer.

title: Google Auth Migration
summary: Replace username/password auth with Google OAuth and eventually decommission password-based login entirely.

title: Analytics, Logging, and Observability
summary: Per-user interaction logging, GA frontend tracking, and OpenTelemetry instrumentation in a separate analytics DB. Storage choice TBD.

title: Everycent MCP Server
summary: Build an MCP server as the reporting/querying layer. Users ask natural language questions via any MCP client. Possible alternative transaction ingestion path. Likely built in Go.
outcome_note: Merged into natural-language-querying-nlq-for-everycent as an architectural component (thin translation layer above Rails /api/mcp/ endpoints). No longer tracked as a separate idea.

title: Periodic refactoring process and /refactor skill
summary: Before each feature migration, run a structured refactoring pass: inventory shared patterns, identify extraction candidates, propose changes, apply and test. Process needs to be tried 2-3 times manually before extracting into a /refactor skill. Too vague to scope yet — try the process out first and see what works and doesn't.

title: Inline code review tooling (no MR)
summary: When doing code cleanup without a PR, it would be useful to annotate lines of code directly like a typical code review. Either explore existing tooling or eventually build a code review extension for this purpose.

title: Everycent mobile app
summary: Build a mobile app for everycent as a separate codebase/project. Key challenge: getting transactions from a bank's mobile app/website into everycent. On desktop, copy-paste works. Need to ideate on viable options for mobile transaction import.

title: Domus task commit workflow
summary: Domus task and idea files sit in .domus/ and are tracked by git, but there's no clear convention for when to commit them. They can drift uncommitted for long periods. Need to figure out the right trigger points — e.g. end of session, when a task transitions to done, when a spec is finalised — and whether they should be batched with related code commits or kept separate.

title: Doctor Prototype: Full Vue 3 Codebase Review
summary: Use a comprehensive webclientv4 code review as the test case for prototyping the Domus Doctor role — capture the process to make it reproducible.

title: Natural Language Querying (NLQ) for Everycent
summary: Enable users to query their finance data in plain English using LLM + pgvector + SQL. Requires new infrastructure (pgvector, embeddings, MCP server) but unlocks powerful ad-hoc analysis.

title: Payee Name Backfill for Historical Transactions
summary: Retroactively match historical transaction descriptions to known payee names using fuzzy/semantic matching with human review. Improves embedding quality for NLQ and general data cleanliness.

title: Hunter: LLM-Powered Bug Reporting & Autonomous Fix Pipeline
summary: A new domus role (hunter) that conducts diagnostic interviews with non-dev users via an in-app chat interface, investigates using source code, server logs, and DB queries, then hands off a well-understood bug to the taskmaster for autonomous fix dispatch.

title: Redesign EverycentApp UI using Google Stitch
summary: After the Vue 3 migration completes, use Google Stitch to generate a fresh UI design — a visual refresh driven by AI-generated high-fidelity screens.

title: Dependency-ordered task display in domus overview
summary: Display tasks in topological (dependency) order within each status category in domus task overview, replacing the separate Blocked section. The ordering itself communicates priority — blocked tasks just appear later in the list.

title: Naming convention for bridge composables vs pure stores
summary: useNotifications wraps PrimeVue toast - need a way to distinguish at a glance which composables are safe to use as real stores in tests vs which need mocking because they bridge external services.

title: Extract buildSettingsStore and similar store factories for test setup
summary: After mock-API migration lands, check if settingsStore/headingStore setup is repeated across page specs. If the 2-line pattern (mock API + flushPromises) appears 3+ times, extract a factory. settingsStore is special — router guard pre-loads it, so page tests might not need to set it up themselves.

title: Clean up domus task list display and status sync
Auto-generate TypeScript types from Rails API
summary: Derive frontend TypeScript type definitions from the Rails API's actual serializer output, eliminating hand-maintained parallel type definitions and guaranteeing FE/BE type alignment.

title: Decide auth migration direction off devise_token_auth
summary: devise_token_auth is dormant and blocks Rails 8; decide whether to keep password login (keep devise + devise-jwt) or go Google-only (drop devise, owned opaque bearer tokens). The fork hinges on the password-login decision.
